steps.title: Find the new side length
steps.content:
Square P has perimeter $4x$. Square Q has perimeter $208$ inches more, so its perimeter is $4x+208$. Divide by $4$ to get the side length of square Q:
$$\frac{4x+208}{4}=x+52$$

steps.title: Square the side length
steps.content:
The area of a square is its side length squared, so the function is:
$$f(x)=(x+52)^2$$The answer is A.
